summary: This cluster of research focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, integrating molecular phylogenetics with detailed morphological analysis to resolve species boundaries and evolutionary relationships. A key finding was the distinction of the subgenus *Halamphora Cleve* as a separate lineage, supporting a proposed reclassification of the genus, and the identification of *Amphora terroris* as a new species. The studies collectively emphasize that morphological data remain critical for taxonomic revision within this group, even when informed by molecular markers. Geographically, the research spans diverse marine and estuarine environments globally, reflecting the widespread distribution of *Amphora* species.
